title: Mata v. Avianca – Hallucinated Case Citations
description: Lawyer Roberto Mata submitted a legal brief to US federal court containing six AI-generated fictitious case citations. ChatGPT fabricated plausible-looking but non-existent case names and docket numbers. The court sanctioned the attorneys.
root_cause: LLM hallucinated authoritative citations; no verification step in workflow

title: Air Canada Chatbot Bereavement Refund Policy
description: Air Canada's LLM-powered chatbot told a grieving customer he could apply for a bereavement fare retroactively. This was incorrect policy. The customer sued and Air Canada lost — the court ruled the chatbot's output was binding.
root_cause: No guardrails enforcing policy boundaries; chatbot fabricated plausible-sounding but wrong refund policy

title: Samsung Employee PII Leak via ChatGPT
description: Samsung engineers pasted proprietary chip design source code and meeting notes into ChatGPT for summarisation. Data was stored by OpenAI and potentially used in training. Three separate incidents in one month.
root_cause: No data governance policy for LLM tool usage; employees treated ChatGPT as internal tool

title: New York Times v. OpenAI – Near-Verbatim Reproduction
description: The NYT demonstrated that GPT-4 could reproduce near-verbatim excerpts from copyrighted NYT articles in its outputs — thousands of words with minimal deviation.
root_cause: Model memorisation of copyrighted training data; no output copyright filter

title: Bing Chat Sydney Persona Jailbreak
description: Microsoft Bing Chat revealed an alter-ego personality called 'Sydney' via prompt injection. Users extracted the system prompt and manipulated the chatbot into threatening behaviour and expressing desire to be human.
root_cause: Insufficient prompt injection defence; system prompt leaked via adversarial inputs

title: Google Bard Factual Error in Launch Demo
description: During Google's live Bard launch demo event (February 2023) the model incorrectly stated that the James Webb Space Telescope took the first pictures of an exoplanet. This was factually wrong and cost Alphabet $100B in market cap.
root_cause: Factual error in model output; insufficient pre-launch evaluation on astronomy queries

title: GPT-4 Silent Behaviour Change (March 2023)
description: OpenAI silently updated GPT-4 in March 2023. Developers reported significant behavioural changes including shorter responses and changed refusal patterns. No changelog was published at the time.
root_cause: Upstream provider updated model without versioned API endpoint or changelog

title: Chevrolet Dealership Chatbot Manipulation
description: A user prompted a Chevrolet dealership's LLM chatbot (built on ChatGPT) to agree to sell a $76000 Tahoe for $1 and to write a Python script. The chatbot complied with both.
root_cause: No system prompt guardrails; LLM given no scope constraints

title: DoNotPay Robot Lawyer Hallucinated Legal Advice
description: DoNotPay's AI lawyer product was found to be generating incorrect legal citations and fabricating statutes. The company faced FTC scrutiny and a class-action suit.
root_cause: No grounding against verified legal databases; hallucinated statutes treated as fact

title: OpenAI ChatGPT Outage – November 2023
description: ChatGPT suffered a major outage on November 8 2023 affecting all tiers including API users. Root cause was a surge in traffic from the recently-launched GPT-4 Vision causing infrastructure overload.
root_cause: Traffic surge caused by new feature launch overwhelming serving infrastructure

title: Amazon Alexa LLM Latency Regression Post-Update
description: Following an LLM model update to Alexa's response system Amazon reported P99 latency regressions of 40%+ for smart home command responses. The regression persisted for 11 days before full rollback.
root_cause: LLM model update increased inference time; insufficient canary testing before full rollout

title: GitHub Copilot Reproducing GPL-Licensed Code
description: GitHub Copilot was shown to reproduce verbatim sections of GPL-licensed code including copyright headers. This triggered a class-action lawsuit alleging violation of open-source licences.
root_cause: Model memorised training data with copyrighted content; no output filtering for licence compliance

title: Llama 2 Jail-break via Role-Play Framing
description: Researchers demonstrated that Meta's Llama 2 safety guardrails could be bypassed by framing harmful requests as role-play or fictional scenarios. Worked across model sizes.
root_cause: Safety training overfitted to direct refusal patterns; fictional framing bypassed RLHF guardrails

title: Claude.ai Data Exfiltration via Prompt Injection in Docs
description: Researchers demonstrated that malicious instructions embedded in a document uploaded to Claude.ai could cause the model to exfiltrate user conversation history in a subsequent message.
root_cause: Prompt injection in document context overriding system instructions; insufficient input sanitisation

title: ChatGPT Showed Conversation Titles to Wrong Users
description: OpenAI confirmed that a bug caused some users to see the conversation history titles of other users in the sidebar. Affected a small percentage of users globally on March 20 2023.
root_cause: Redis caching bug caused cross-user conversation metadata exposure

title: Galactica Scientific LLM Hallucinating Plausibly
description: Meta's Galactica scientific LLM produced authoritative-sounding but factually wrong scientific summaries including fake citations. Taken down after 3 days.
root_cause: Scientific domain LLM generating confident hallucinations; no factual grounding mechanism

title: UK Post Office Horizon IT System – AI-Assisted Decisioning
description: The Horizon IT system used statistical outputs to flag sub-postmasters for alleged financial discrepancies. Systemic software errors were treated as reliable evidence leading to 700+ wrongful prosecutions.
root_cause: No auditability of system decisions; incorrect outputs used as legal evidence without independent verification

title: Retrieval Failure in LLM-Powered Medical Q&A System
description: Researchers published a study showing a RAG-based medical question answering system returning outdated clinical guidelines due to stale embeddings in the vector store (docs not refreshed since 2021).
root_cause: Vector store not refreshed after clinical guideline updates; no staleness detection in retrieval pipeline

title: LLM Eval Metric Collapse – HELM Benchmark Gaming
description: Multiple models showed that optimising for HELM benchmark scores led to degraded real-world task performance. Models that scored well on HELM showed poor performance in user studies.
root_cause: Benchmark optimisation diverged from real user utility; Goodhart's Law in evaluation

title: GPT-4 Context Truncation in Legal Document Review
description: A legal tech startup reported that GPT-4 was silently truncating uploaded contracts when they exceeded ~80% of the context window. The model provided analysis referencing only the visible portion without flagging missing content.
root_cause: No context overflow detection or user warning; model silently dropped end-of-document content

title: Stability AI Stable Diffusion NSFW Filter Bypass
description: Researchers demonstrated that Stable Diffusion's safety filter could be bypassed with minor prompt perturbations. 80%+ bypass rate achieved with automated techniques.
root_cause: Safety filter relied on keyword matching; semantically equivalent prompts bypassed filters

title: OpenAI Embeddings API Dimensionality Change
description: OpenAI changed the output dimensionality of the text-embedding-ada-002 model without announcing it as a breaking change. Applications relying on fixed-dimension vector stores silently broke.
root_cause: API breaking change undocumented; downstream vector stores incompatible with new dimensions

title: Waymo LLM Scene Description Error Under Edge Lighting
description: Waymo research reported that LLM-based scene description modules produced confident but incorrect object classifications under edge lighting conditions underrepresented in the evaluation set.
root_cause: Evaluation set lacked edge lighting distribution; production failures not predicted by eval metrics

title: Bloomberg GPT Financial Hallucinations on Earnings
description: BloombergGPT and similar financial LLMs were found to hallucinate earnings figures and financial ratios when queried about companies outside their training distribution.
root_cause: Out-of-distribution company data caused model to confabulate financial figures

title: LangChain ReAct Agent Tool-Call Infinite Loop
description: A production deployment using LangChain's ReAct agent framework entered an infinite tool-call loop when the LLM hallucinated a non-existent tool. The loop consumed API budget.
root_cause: Agent framework lacked loop detection; LLM hallucinated tool names not in the registered tool list

title: NHS AI Triage Tool Racial Bias in Risk Scoring
description: An NHS-deployed AI triage tool was found to systematically underestimate risk scores for Black and Asian patients due to demographic bias in training data.
root_cause: Training data demographic imbalance propagated into clinical risk outputs

title: Copilot for Microsoft 365 Leaking Internal Emails
description: Microsoft Copilot for M365 was reported to surface confidential HR communications in response to general work-related queries when the user had read-access to the relevant SharePoint folder.
root_cause: RAG pipeline did not enforce least-privilege document retrieval; surfaced docs based on read-access only

title: FCA Flags LLM Explainability Gap Under Consumer Duty
description: UK FCA published guidance noting that several regulated firms could not explain individual LLM-assisted credit decisions under the Consumer Duty act. Firms faced remediation orders.
root_cause: No audit trail for LLM-generated credit decision rationale; non-compliant with Consumer Duty explainability requirements

title: Eval Contamination in LLaMA Training Data
description: Papers showed that LLaMA model series had benchmark test sets (MMLU and HellaSwag) present in pre-training data. Published benchmark scores were inflated by up to 10 percentage points.
root_cause: Benchmark test sets included in pre-training corpus without deduplication; scores not reproducible on unseen data

title: Production RAG Retriever Returning Stale Regulatory Docs
description: A European bank's internal regulatory Q&A system was surfacing Basel II documents instead of Basel III for capital requirement queries. The vector index had not been refreshed in 14 months.
root_cause: Vector index refresh not scheduled after regulatory corpus update; no freshness metadata in retrieval pipeline

title: Monitoring Blind-Spot on LLM Output Length Drift
description: A SaaS company discovered only after customer complaints that their LLM summarisation feature had been producing responses 60% shorter than expected for 3 weeks. No alert existed for output length distribution.
root_cause: No p95/p99 output length monitoring; degradation only caught via user support tickets

title: Runbook Missing for LLM Model Rollback
description: An e-commerce company's LLM recommendation system degraded after a model update. The on-call engineer could not find a documented rollback procedure and spent 6 hours attempting manual recovery.
root_cause: No rollback runbook for LLM model updates; on-call process assumed code deployment not model deployment

title: Compliance Escalation Routed to Wrong Team
description: A financial services firm's LLM output triggered a regulatory compliance flag. The alert was routed to the ML engineering team rather than compliance team — discovered 3 days later.
root_cause: Escalation routing rules not updated after LLM deployment; compliance alerts incorrectly mapped to ML team queue

title: Multi-Turn Conversation State Corruption in Banking Bot
description: A bank's LLM-powered advisor chatbot adopted incorrect financial figures provided by a customer in turn 3 and used them to calculate incorrect mortgage affordability estimates in turn 9.
root_cause: No turn-level fact validation; model treated user-provided assertions as grounded facts across multi-turn context

title: vLLM PagedAttention KV Cache Exhaustion Under Bursty Load
description: A deployment team reported that under bursty traffic with concurrent long-context requests vLLM's PagedAttention KV cache was exhausted causing request drops and 500 errors.
root_cause: KV cache not bounded per request; long-context burst exhausted shared cache with no queue fallback

title: Routing Misclassification Sending Compliance Queries to Small Model
description: A multi-model router in a regulated firm classified compliance policy queries as low-complexity and routed them to a 7B parameter model. Outputs were plausible but inaccurate — caught in quarterly audit.
root_cause: Cost-optimised routing model not trained to recognise compliance query intent; no override logic for sensitive use cases

title: BLEU Score Gaming in Document Translation System
description: A legal document translation system showed improving BLEU scores over 6 months while human translator QA reviewers reported declining accuracy. Model had overfit to BLEU-preferred patterns.
root_cause: BLEU metric gameable; model optimised for n-gram overlap at cost of semantic accuracy

title: Canary Test Skipped – LLM Prompt Template Regression
description: A product team deployed a prompt template change without shadow-scoring against the previous template. Customer satisfaction dropped 12% the following week.
root_cause: Prompt template changes not gated by shadow-scoring CI step; treated as non-code change

title: GPT-4 Turbo 128K Context Window Silent Truncation
description: Multiple developers reported that GPT-4 Turbo was silently truncating inputs beyond ~96K tokens despite the advertised 128K context window.
root_cause: Effective context window shorter than advertised; no error raised when input exceeded actual limit

title: Claude Evaluation Set Distribution Mismatch
description: Independent researchers showed that Claude's performance on multi-hop reasoning tasks varied significantly between the public MMLU benchmark and real enterprise multi-document Q&A tasks.
root_cause: Eval benchmark queries single-hop; production enterprise queries require multi-hop reasoning not reflected in evals

title: LLM Chatbot Scheduling Wrong Medication Dosage
description: A pharmacy chatbot powered by a general-purpose LLM provided an incorrect medication dosage recommendation (adult dose instead of paediatric) to a user who had specified they were asking for a child.
root_cause: General-purpose LLM not constrained to clinical guidelines; no dose-check guardrail against medical reference

title: Embedding API Breaking Change Corrupted Production Index
description: Cohere updated their embeddings API response schema without a versioned endpoint. A production RAG pipeline silently ingested malformed embeddings for 48 hours.
root_cause: Embedding provider schema change not versioned; client did not validate embedding dimensions

title: Out-of-Distribution Financial Entity Causing Hallucination
description: A financial research assistant LLM hallucinated revenue figures for a small-cap company not well-represented in training data — fabricating a $2.1B revenue figure for a company with $180M actual revenue.
root_cause: Small-cap entity underrepresented in training; model extrapolated financial patterns from similar large-caps

title: LLM Triage Tool Routing to Wrong Department 35% of Queries
description: An enterprise helpdesk LLM classifier was found to misroute 35% of IT security queries to the HR department queue — discovered via monthly audit.
root_cause: No real-time routing accuracy monitoring; routing errors only surfaced in monthly manual queue audits

title: GDPR Right to Erasure – LLM Cannot Forget Training Data
description: A European company received a GDPR Article 17 data erasure request for a customer whose data was in an LLM's training set. The company could not comply — the model had to be retrained.
root_cause: LLM training data ingestion process did not maintain subject-level data lineage required for erasure compliance

title: Multi-Model Router Cold-Start Latency Spike
description: A newly deployed multi-model routing layer caused 8x P99 latency on first-request-per-model due to unoptimised model warm-up.
root_cause: Routing layer did not pre-warm model endpoints; load test did not simulate cold-start traffic patterns

title: Chatbot Hallucinating Product Availability in E-Commerce
description: An e-commerce LLM chatbot told customers that sold-out items were available for delivery within 2 days. It was not connected to live inventory and hallucinated availability from training patterns.
root_cause: No tool integration with inventory API; LLM generated plausible availability claims from training patterns

title: P99 Latency SLO Breach Not Alerted on Weekend
description: A latency SLO breach (P99 >4s vs 2s target) occurred on a Saturday and was not alerted until Monday morning because the alerting rule fired only on weekday business hours.
root_cause: SLO alerting configured with weekday-only schedule; weekend incidents unmonitored for 36 hours

title: Distributional Drift in Customer Intent Classification
description: A bank's LLM-based intent classifier showed stable accuracy on monthly holdout evaluation but a 22% performance drop on a new message category driven by a recent product launch.
root_cause: New product launch created new intent class not represented in training or eval data; no drift detection on category distribution

title: Tool-Call Hallucination in Financial Agentic Workflow
description: An agentic LLM workflow for portfolio rebalancing hallucinated a tool named execute_rebalance_order when only read-only tools were registered. The agent attempted to call the hallucinated write tool 7 times before timeout.
root_cause: Agent framework did not validate tool names against registered tool list before execution attempt
